title: Inspect rendered state before claiming visual correctness
statement: An agent must not infer visible UI correctness from source text alone; it needs direct inspection of the rendered target before explaining or claiming success.
rationale: Source can be correct while runtime composition, state, density, or stale assets remain visibly wrong.

prefer: Inspect the current rendered target in the relevant state and viewport.
Report what was observed separately from what source implies.
avoid: Declaring UI correct because code looks correct.
Using stale screenshots or an unverified server.

when: An agent explains, reviews, or claims completion of visible UI work.
not_when: The task is explicitly source-only and visual state is out of scope, which must be stated.

evidence.summary: Questioned an agent's claim about rendered content and requested a canvas screenshot capability.
Reported a valid callout existed on disk but was absent from the rendered canvas because inspection never checked the visual result.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Inspect the current rendered target in the relevant state and viewport.
Report what was observed separately from what source implies.
negative_examples: Declaring UI correct because code looks correct.
Using stale screenshots or an unverified server.

checks: Was the current rendered target inspected directly?
Were the relevant state and viewport exercised?
Does the report distinguish observed behavior from inference?
